Make sure to search and apply for a trademark on a website called USPTO.gov. USPTO.gov. On that site, which is completely free, you can search anything you need about a trademark or a patent. The trademark is very, very easy to search because you just go inside. There's a site link in there, T-E-S-S, within there in that trademark search database. Let's say you want to trademark the name. Who's your daddy? Or Victory Poker? Or one of those fun names that I've had over my lifetime. But they were very famous names. So I had to go in and search for certain segments that would work specifically for me. And then trademark everything around that. With Who's Daddy, we got a dozen trademarks for 300 different products, all different classes. With Victory Poker, we had to go and research because a lot of people liked the name Victory. There was Victory Sports, there was Victory Furniture, Victory Plates, and Victory... There were so many things with the word Victory. I had to go in there and trademark Victory Poker for online commerce, for clothing, for accessories. I made it for CDs and for mouse pads. Anything I thought that would fit, I trademarked that. That way, I had all my ducks in a row. So it's very important, go to USPTO.gov, research your name, look it up. A lot of times, people are going to have trademarks around the segment that you want to be in. Just make sure you're not competing directly with them. When you apply, the trademark attorney is normally going to tell you if there's going to be a way for them to battle with you, or to fight you ahead of time. So you're not going to be spending thousands and thousands of dollars to get something. Most of the time, you can do the search for free. Pay them a few hundred dollars to do an actual in-depth search to see if somebody's going to be able to stop you. You're not going to be able to start a clothing line called Coca-Cola. Just because you think Coca-Cola is a beverage, they still trademarked it for clothing and mouse pads and electrical equipment. Everything you could think of just so nobody was sneaky or smart and tried to come in and trademark for a certain segment. So you're not going to be able to get names that are battling with Ford or somebody like that. They're going to have almost every single division. The main way that somebody like a judge decides whether or not you're infringing on a trademark is if an 11-year-old was looking at Coca-Cola shirts versus Coca-Cola the drink and they can't determine what's the difference, you lose. So don't go out there and try to battle or try to get a trademark against somebody very, very big because if anybody was going to like Apple or Amazon or the San Diego Chargers, if you try to get a name that's similar to any one of these big corporations, they're going to be able to stop you. So do those searches ahead of time, spend the time on it, it's free, and then have an attorney go in for a few hundred dollars and really dive in to see if they can trademark a name for you before you waste a lot of money ahead of time.
